intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gentle, approachable UI for health tracking over sharp clinical aesthetics
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use generous border-radius, pill-shaped buttons, curved cards to feel supportive rather than clinical
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer sharper, more minimal aesthetic or clinical precision to convey data seriousness

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer gentle, approachable UI for health tracking over sharp clinical aesthetics
evidence.implication: Dashboard should use generous border-radius, pill-shaped buttons, curved cards to feel supportive rather than clinical

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, lightweight language over clinical health terminology
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should feel like a friend asking 'how are you?' rather than a doctor. Use contractions, simple words, warmth.
facade.rejectImplies: Voice should be more clinical, authoritative, data-forward. Use formal health/medical language. Remove personality.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, eased animations between dashboard states over instant jumps
facade.label: Smooth transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into spring physics, ease curves, and choreographed sequencing for metric updates and state changes
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant rendering, snap updates, and minimal or no animation—speed and clarity over motion

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments want a calm, organic visual foundation that feels grounded rather than sterile or digital
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + organic vs digital
facade.label: Muted earth tones
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use warm browns, sage greens, soft terracottas—natural palette that reduces visual stress during data review
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers either cooler tones (blues, grays) or more saturated/vibrant colors; dashboard should feel more clinical or energetic, less natural

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer left sidebar for experiment list + horizontal tabs for different views (overview, timeline, data) over a single unified scrolling dashboard
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation architecture: sidebar + tabs vs full-width tabbed interface vs single scrolling view
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs below
facade.acceptImplies: Explore modular, zone-based layouts where navigation and view-switching are separated; test sticky sidebars; consider collapsible navigation
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward integrated navigation; test full-width designs; explore tab-in-header patterns or single scrolling canvas; prioritize sequential over spatial organization

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer seeing a single dominant metric with minimal context over dense narrative explanations
facade.axisTargeted: Charts vs text — specifically, do users want one bold number + brief label, or supporting narrative context?
facade.label: Big number, small story
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward metric-first design: large KPI displays with micro-copy. Suggests dashboard should lead with numbers, relegate explanations to tooltips or expandable sections.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants narrative framing: each number needs context, meaning, and story. Suggests dashboard should pair metrics with annotations, trend descriptions, or human-readable insights.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users feel more motivated and less burdened by dashboards with bright backgrounds, ample whitespace, and minimal visual weight
facade.label: Light and airy
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use light color palettes (whites, soft neutrals), generous spacing, thin typography, and minimal shadows to create an open, breathing feel
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer darker or more densely packed interfaces; dashboard could use richer backgrounds, tighter layouts, or higher visual complexity without feeling oppressive

draft.title: Health Experiment Tracker
draft.summary: A personal dashboard for monitoring long-term health experiments with experiment cards, metrics, and progress visualization.
draft.html:
Health Experiments

Track your long-term health initiatives

Active Experiments

Sleep Optimization

Active

Testing consistent 10 PM bedtime for 8 weeks

Week 5 of 8 62% complete
Avg Sleep Duration 7.2 hrs
Sleep Quality 7.8/10

Morning Hydration

Active

Drinking 500ml water within 30 min of waking

Week 3 of 8 38% complete
Consistency 85%
Days Logged 18 of 21
Completed Experiments

Caffeine Cutoff

Completed

No caffeine after 2 PM for 4 weeks

Week 4 of 4 100% complete
